title: insider_credential_misuse
description: An employee who has submitted their resignation is detected accessing and exfiltrating sensitive HR and financial records outside business hours using their valid credentials.

timeline.title: After-Hours Access Alert
timeline.description: DLP and UEBA systems alert on an employee accessing large volumes of HR and financial records at 11 PM — outside normal working hours.

timeline.title: Bulk Data Download Detected
timeline.description: The employee is downloading bulk employee records to an external USB storage device.

timeline.title: Confidential Data Emailed Externally
timeline.description: DLP detects the employee forwarding files containing confidential salary and performance data to a personal email address.

timeline.title: Access Revoked
timeline.description: HR and Security jointly authorise immediate revocation of the employee's system access.

timeline.title: Legal and HR Notified
timeline.description: Legal counsel and HR leadership briefed. Evidence preserved for potential disciplinary and legal proceedings.

timeline.title: Insider Threat Controls Reviewed
timeline.description: Offboarding process updated and user behaviour analytics expanded to cover all resignation-risk employees.

node-1.title: After-Hours DLP Alert Fires
node-1.situation: At 11 PM, DLP and User and Entity Behaviour Analytics (UEBA) systems alert on an employee who submitted their resignation two days ago. The employee is accessing large volumes of HR records and financial documents — far outside their normal access patterns.
node-1.question: What is your first action?

options.label: A
options.text: Escalate immediately to security and HR management, preserve all audit logs, and confirm the activity is not authorised before taking further steps.

options.label: B
options.text: Ignore the alert — the employee still has valid credentials and may be catching up on work.

options.label: C
options.text: Immediately disable the employee's account without notifying HR or management.

node-2.title: Data Exfiltration to USB Confirmed
node-2.situation: Security and HR confirm the access is not authorised. Logs show the employee is copying bulk employee records — including personal contact information and salary data — to a personal USB drive plugged into their workstation.
node-2.question: How do you respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Revoke the employee's system access with joint authorisation from HR and Security, and preserve all evidence without alerting the employee.

options.label: B
options.text: Confront the employee directly via instant message before taking any account action.

node-3.title: Emails Sent to Personal Account
node-3.situation: DLP logs reveal the employee also forwarded files containing confidential salary bands, performance reviews, and an upcoming redundancy list to a personal Gmail address before access was revoked. Potential data breach regulations may apply.
node-3.question: How do you respond?

options.label: A
options.text: Notify legal counsel, conduct a data breach assessment to determine if regulatory notification is required, and engage law enforcement if the threshold is met.

options.label: B
options.text: Delete the outgoing emails from the mail server and consider the incident contained.

node-4.title: Strengthening Insider Threat Controls
node-4.situation: The immediate incident is contained. Legal and HR are managing the employee situation. Leadership wants to prevent a similar insider threat incident in the future.
node-4.question: What controls do you recommend?

options.label: A
options.text: Implement a formal offboarding process that revokes access on the day of resignation notice, enforce least-privilege access policies, and expand UEBA monitoring to cover all employees flagged as resignation-risk.

options.label: B
options.text: Add a policy reminder to the employee handbook about acceptable data use.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The employee's access was revoked and all evidence preserved. Legal counsel has been engaged and a data breach assessment is underway. An updated offboarding process now ensures access is revoked on the day of resignation, and UEBA monitoring has been expanded across the organisation.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
